# Bitter Roots, Rich Flavor

## Scripture

"Now faith is the substance of things hoped for, the evidence of things not seen." - Hebrews 11:1 (ESV)

## Reflection

Just as a pour-over coffee brew requires carefully balanced temperatures, steeping times, and the right ratio of water to coffee grounds, our walk with God demands a delicate balance of faith and trust. Like the bitter roots of a coffee plant, our faith may seem unpalatable at first, but it yields a rich flavor of hope and assurance when nurtured through prayer, scripture, and obedience. Just as the crema forms on a perfectly extracted espresso, our trust in God's goodness and sovereignty is refined through trials and tribulations. As we begin each day with a morning ritual, so too should we start with a conscious decision to steep our hearts in the Word and allow our faith to be the substance of our every waking moment.

## Prayer

We thank you, Lord, for the gift of faith that yields hope and assurance. Help us to nurture our trust in you, even when the road ahead is uncertain.

## One-Line Application
May our daily disciplines cultivate a rich flavor of faith, yielding hope and assurance in every circumstance.
